modules.title: SELECT
modules.description: Project data from a single relation with deliberate column choices.

exercises.learning_objective: Select named columns from a relation.
exercises.statement: Return each active product's product_id, sku, name, and unit_price.
exercises.ordering: Order by product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use DISTINCT to remove repeated values.
exercises.statement: Return the distinct payment methods that have been used by completed payments.
exercises.ordering: Order alphabetically by payment_method.

exercises.learning_objective: Alias projected columns for a clear result contract.
exercises.statement: Return each warehouse's code as warehouse_code, name as warehouse_name, and capacity_units as capacity.
exercises.ordering: Order by warehouse_code ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Project derived values with arithmetic expressions.
exercises.statement: For every order item, return order_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price, and the line total (quantity multiplied by unit_price) as line_total.
exercises.ordering: Order by order_id, then product_id.

exercises.learning_objective: Select a complete row shape only when it is the required contract.
exercises.statement: Return all columns for countries in the reference data.
exercises.ordering: Order by country_code ascending.

modules.title: WHERE
modules.description: Filter rows with predicates, ranges, and boolean conditions.

exercises.learning_objective: Filter rows using an equality predicate.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, email, first_name, and last_name for active customers.
exercises.ordering: Order by customer_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Filter numeric values with a comparison predicate.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, sku, name, and unit_price for active products priced above 1,000.00.
exercises.ordering: Order by unit_price ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine predicates with AND.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, customer_id, ordered_at, and status for delivered orders placed during 2024.
exercises.ordering: Order by ordered_at ascending, then order_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Filter values with IN.
exercises.statement: Return payment_id, order_id, payment_method, amount, and status for completed payments made by card or wallet.
exercises.ordering: Order by payment_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Compare one column to another in a row predicate.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, product_id, quantity_on_hand, and reorder_point for inventory rows at or below their reorder point.
exercises.ordering: Order by warehouse_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

modules.title: ORDER BY
modules.description: Control result ordering with columns, directions, and tie-breakers.

exercises.learning_objective: Sort a result by one column in descending order.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, name, and unit_price for active products.
exercises.ordering: Order by unit_price descending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Sort text values alphabetically with a deterministic tie-breaker.
exercises.statement: Return supplier_id, name, and contact_email for all suppliers.
exercises.ordering: Order by name ascending, then supplier_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Sort timestamps from newest to oldest.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, customer_id, ordered_at, and status for all orders.
exercises.ordering: Order by ordered_at descending, then order_id descending.

exercises.learning_objective: Order by a computed expression.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price, and line_total (quantity multiplied by unit_price) for all order items.
exercises.ordering: Order by line_total descending, then order_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Control the placement of NULL values while sorting.
exercises.statement: Return employee_id, first_name, last_name, and manager_id for all employees.
exercises.ordering: Order by manager_id ascending with NULL manager_id values last, then employee_id ascending.

modules.title: Expressions
modules.description: Derive values with arithmetic, aliases, and boolean expressions.

exercises.learning_objective: Create a numeric expression and name its result.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price, and line_total (quantity multiplied by unit_price) for every order item.
exercises.ordering: Order by order_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use arithmetic to calculate a difference.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, product_id, quantity_on_hand, reorder_point, and units_above_reorder (quantity_on_hand minus reorder_point) for all inventory rows.
exercises.ordering: Order by warehouse_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine multiple columns into a derived total.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, shipping_fee, and shipping_fee plus 18 percent tax as shipping_fee_with_tax for every order.
exercises.ordering: Order by order_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Create a boolean expression in the SELECT list.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, name, unit_price, and a boolean is_premium that is true when unit_price is at least 1,000.00.
exercises.ordering: Order by product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a scalar expression to convert a monetary amount to another unit.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, sku, unit_price, and price_in_cents, where price_in_cents is unit_price multiplied by 100.
exercises.ordering: Order by product_id ascending.

modules.title: NULL
modules.description: Handle missing values correctly with null-aware predicates and expressions.

exercises.learning_objective: Find missing values with IS NULL.
exercises.statement: Return employee_id, first_name, last_name, and manager_id for employees who do not have a manager.
exercises.ordering: Order by employee_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Find present values with IS NOT NULL.
exercises.statement: Return shipment_id, order_id, shipped_at, and delivered_at for shipments that have been delivered.
exercises.ordering: Order by shipment_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Substitute a fallback value with COALESCE.
exercises.statement: Return shipment_id, order_id, delivered_at, and latest_known_event_at. Use delivered_at when available; otherwise use shipped_at.
exercises.ordering: Order by shipment_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a NULL condition together with another predicate.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, customer_id, ordered_at, and status for cancelled orders that have no promotion applied.
exercises.ordering: Order by order_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use COALESCE to produce a display-ready text value.
exercises.statement: Return review_id, product_id, customer_id, rating, and review_text. Replace a NULL review body with the text No written review.
exercises.ordering: Order by review_id ascending.

modules.title: Aggregate Functions
modules.description: Summarize sets of rows with COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX.

exercises.learning_objective: Use conditional aggregate filters to count multiple subsets in one result.
exercises.statement: Return active_customer_count, suspended_customer_count, and closed_customer_count: the number of customers in each respective status.
exercises.ordering: The result has one row; no ordering is needed.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine COUNT, SUM, and AVG after filtering the input rows.
exercises.statement: For completed payments only, return completed_payment_count, completed_payment_total, and average_completed_payment_amount. Round the average amount to two decimal places.
exercises.ordering: The result has one row; no ordering is needed.

exercises.learning_objective: Aggregate multiple columns and derive a value from aggregate results.
exercises.statement: Return total_units_on_hand, total_reorder_point, and units_above_reorder across all inventory rows. units_above_reorder is the sum of quantity_on_hand minus reorder_point only for rows where quantity_on_hand is greater than reorder_point; do not let below-reorder rows reduce this value.
exercises.ordering: The result has one row; no ordering is needed.

exercises.learning_objective: Produce a compact statistical summary from filtered rows.
exercises.statement: For active products only, return active_product_count, lowest_active_product_price, highest_active_product_price, and average_active_product_price. Round the average price to two decimal places.
exercises.ordering: The result has one row; no ordering is needed.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine date filtering with timestamp and numeric aggregates.
exercises.statement: For orders placed during 2024, return first_ordered_at, last_ordered_at, order_count, and average_shipping_fee. Round the average shipping fee to two decimal places.
exercises.ordering: The result has one row; no ordering is needed.

modules.title: GROUP BY
modules.description: Produce one aggregate result per group of related rows.

exercises.learning_objective: Calculate multiple aggregates for each categorical group.
exercises.statement: Return status, order_count, and total_shipping_fee: the number of orders and the sum of shipping_fee for each order status.
exercises.ordering: Order by total_shipping_fee descending, then status ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Group rows by a foreign-key value while summarizing a date range.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, order_count, first_ordered_at, and last_ordered_at for each customer who has placed at least one order.
exercises.ordering: Order by order_count descending, then customer_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Summarize multiple inventory measures per group.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, total_units_on_hand, total_reorder_point, and units_above_reorder. For each warehouse, units_above_reorder is the sum of quantity_on_hand minus reorder_point only for rows where quantity_on_hand is greater than reorder_point; below-reorder rows must not reduce this value.
exercises.ordering: Order by units_above_reorder ascending, then warehouse_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine COUNT and a rounded AVG, then sort by aggregate results.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, review_count, and average_rating for each reviewed product. average_rating must be rounded to two decimal places.
exercises.ordering: Order by average_rating descending, then review_count descending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Filter rows before grouping and calculate multiple aggregates for each group.
exercises.statement: For active products only, return supplier_id, active_product_count, and average_active_product_price. Round average_active_product_price to two decimal places.
exercises.ordering: Order by average_active_product_price descending, then active_product_count descending, then supplier_id ascending.

modules.title: HAVING
modules.description: Filter aggregate groups using conditions evaluated after GROUP BY.

exercises.learning_objective: Keep only groups whose row count meets a threshold.
exercises.statement: Return payment_method and completed_payment_count for payment methods with at least 100 completed payments.
exercises.ordering: Order by completed_payment_count descending, then payment_method ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use HAVING to filter grouped sums.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id and total_units_on_hand for warehouses whose total quantity_on_hand is greater than 50,000.
exercises.ordering: Order by total_units_on_hand descending, then warehouse_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Filter groups using an aggregate of a derived expression.
exercises.statement: Return product_id and total_sales_value for products whose total value across all order items exceeds 25,000.00. Compute each line's value as quantity multiplied by unit_price.
exercises.ordering: Order by total_sales_value descending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine WHERE and HAVING so row filters and group filters occur at the correct stages.
exercises.statement: Return supplier_id and active_product_count for suppliers with at least 10 active products. Exclude inactive products before forming groups.
exercises.ordering: Order by active_product_count descending, then supplier_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Apply multiple aggregate conditions to retain qualifying groups.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, review_count, and average_rating for products that have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating of at least 4.00. Round average_rating to two decimal places.
exercises.ordering: Order by average_rating descending, then review_count descending, then product_id ascending.

modules.title: INNER JOIN
modules.description: Combine related rows that have matching keys in both tables.

exercises.learning_objective: Join two related tables using a foreign key.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, product_name, supplier_name, and unit_price for active products.
exercises.ordering: Order by supplier_name ascending, then product_name ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Join order records to customer details after filtering rows.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, ordered_at, status, customer_id, customer_first_name, and customer_last_name for delivered orders.
exercises.ordering: Order by ordered_at descending, then order_id descending.

exercises.learning_objective: Join order headers to line items and calculate a derived value.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, ordered_at, product_id, quantity, unit_price, and line_total for order items on paid, shipped, or delivered orders. line_total is quantity multiplied by unit_price.
exercises.ordering: Order by ordered_at descending, then order_id descending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine three tables to enrich an event with related names.
exercises.statement: Return review_id, product_name, customer_id, customer_first_name, customer_last_name, rating, and created_at for reviews with a rating of 4 or 5.
exercises.ordering: Order by created_at descending, then review_id descending.

exercises.learning_objective: Traverse several foreign-key relationships in one query.
exercises.statement: Return shipment_id, shipment_status, warehouse_code, warehouse_name, city_name, and country_name for delivered shipments.
exercises.ordering: Order by country_name ascending, city_name ascending, warehouse_code ascending, then shipment_id ascending.

modules.title: OUTER JOIN
modules.description: Preserve unmatched rows while combining optional relationships.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a LEFT JOIN to find rows with no related match.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, email, first_name, and last_name for customers who have never placed an order.
exercises.ordering: Order by customer_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Count optional related rows while retaining rows with no matches.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, product_name, and review_count for every active product, including products with no reviews.
exercises.ordering: Order by review_count descending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Place a related-table filter in the join condition to preserve unmatched rows.
exercises.statement: Return supplier_id, supplier_name, and active_product_count for every supplier, including suppliers with no active products.
exercises.ordering: Order by active_product_count descending, then supplier_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use COALESCE with an outer join to return zero instead of NULL aggregate results.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, warehouse_code, and total_units_on_hand for every warehouse, including warehouses with no inventory rows. Show 0 rather than NULL for total_units_on_hand.
exercises.ordering: Order by total_units_on_hand descending, then warehouse_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Count conditionally matched rows without removing unmatched parent rows.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, product_name, and open_return_count for every active product. Count only returns whose status is requested or approved, and include products with no such returns.
exercises.ordering: Order by open_return_count descending, then product_id ascending.

modules.title: SELF JOIN
modules.description: Join a table to itself to navigate hierarchical and peer relationships.

exercises.learning_objective: Join an employee to its manager in the same table.
exercises.statement: Return employee_id, employee_first_name, employee_last_name, manager_id, manager_first_name, and manager_last_name for employees who have a manager.
exercises.ordering: Order by manager_id ascending, then employee_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a LEFT self join to retain hierarchy roots.
exercises.statement: Return employee_id, employee_first_name, employee_last_name, manager_first_name, and manager_last_name for every employee, including employees who have no manager.
exercises.ordering: Order by employee_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Navigate an optional parent-child hierarchy within one table.
exercises.statement: Return category_id, category_name, parent_category_id, and parent_category_name for every category, including root categories with no parent.
exercises.ordering: Order by parent_category_name ascending with NULL values first, then category_name ascending, then category_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Count child rows per parent while retaining rows with no children.
exercises.statement: Return employee_id, first_name, last_name, and direct_report_count for every employee, including employees with no direct reports.
exercises.ordering: Order by direct_report_count descending, then employee_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Chain self joins to navigate more than one hierarchy level.
exercises.statement: Return employee_id, employee_first_name, employee_last_name, manager_first_name, manager_last_name, grandmanager_first_name, and grandmanager_last_name for employees whose manager also has a manager.
exercises.ordering: Order by employee_id ascending.

modules.title: CROSS JOIN
modules.description: Build deliberate combinations of rows from independent sets.

exercises.learning_objective: Produce every possible pair from two reference tables.
exercises.statement: Return country_code, country_name, warehouse_code, and warehouse_name for every possible country-and-warehouse combination.
exercises.ordering: Order by country_code ascending, then warehouse_code ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Create combinations first, then exclude pairs that do not meet a comparison rule.
exercises.statement: Return store_id, store_code, warehouse_id, and warehouse_code for every store-and-warehouse pair located in different cities.
exercises.ordering: Order by store_id ascending, then warehouse_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Create a planning matrix between two business dimensions.
exercises.statement: Return supplier_id, supplier_name, category_id, and category_name for every possible supplier-and-category combination.
exercises.ordering: Order by supplier_id ascending, then category_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a CROSS JOIN to construct a filtered allocation grid.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, warehouse_code, product_id, sku, and unit_price for every warehouse-and-product combination where the product is active and priced at least 1,000.00.
exercises.ordering: Order by warehouse_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Cross join a table with a derived value set to generate a complete reference matrix.
exercises.statement: Return country_code, country_name, and payment_method for every country-and-payment-method combination. The payment methods are card, wallet, bank_transfer, and cash_on_delivery.
exercises.ordering: Order by country_code ascending, then payment_method ascending.

modules.title: SUBQUERIES
modules.description: Use nested queries to compare rows with derived values and related aggregates.

exercises.learning_objective: Compare each row to a scalar aggregate returned by a subquery.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, sku, name, and unit_price for active products priced above the average unit_price of all active products.
exercises.ordering: Order by unit_price descending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a correlated subquery to compare a row with the average of its group.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, product_name, category_id, and unit_price for active products priced above the average unit_price of active products in the same category.
exercises.ordering: Order by category_id ascending, unit_price descending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Filter parent rows using a subquery that aggregates related rows.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, email, first_name, and last_name for customers who have placed more orders than the average number of orders per customer. Treat the average as the total number of orders divided by the number of distinct customers who placed an order.
exercises.ordering: Order by customer_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a correlated aggregate subquery to identify the top row within each group.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, warehouse_code, product_id, sku, quantity_on_hand, and reorder_point for inventory rows whose quantity_on_hand is the highest in their warehouse. Include every tied highest row.
exercises.ordering: Order by warehouse_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine a correlated subquery with a nested aggregate to compare related business metrics.
exercises.statement: Return supplier_id, supplier_name, product_id, product_name, and unit_price for active products whose unit_price is greater than the average unit_price of that supplier's active products. Include only suppliers that have at least two active products.
exercises.ordering: Order by supplier_id ascending, unit_price descending, then product_id ascending.

modules.title: EXISTS
modules.description: Use existence tests to filter rows based on related data.

exercises.learning_objective: Filter parent rows by the existence of a related row.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, email, first_name, and last_name for customers who have placed at least one order.
exercises.ordering: Order by customer_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use NOT EXISTS to find rows without a related match.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, sku, and name for active products that have never received a review.
exercises.ordering: Order by product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Test for a related row that satisfies an additional condition.
exercises.statement: Return supplier_id, supplier_name, product_id, product_name, and unit_price for active products from suppliers that also supply at least one other active product priced above 1,000.00.
exercises.ordering: Order by supplier_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine joins and an existence test to filter a transaction result.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, ordered_at, customer_id, customer_first_name, customer_last_name, and status for orders that contain at least one product with a unit_price of 1,000.00 or more. Include each qualifying order once.
exercises.ordering: Order by ordered_at descending, then order_id descending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a correlated NOT EXISTS test with multiple prior concepts.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, warehouse_code, product_id, sku, quantity_on_hand, and reorder_point for inventory rows that are at or below their reorder point and have no matching store_inventory row with a positive quantity_on_hand for the same product.
exercises.ordering: Order by warehouse_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

modules.title: IN
modules.description: Match a value against a subquery result set.

exercises.learning_objective: Filter rows using a single-column subquery result.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, sku, name, and unit_price for products supplied by suppliers located in the same country as the customer whose email is 'customer1@example.test'.
exercises.ordering: Order by product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use NOT IN with a non-NULL subquery result to exclude matched rows.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, email, first_name, and last_name for customers who have not placed an order with status paid, shipped, or delivered.
exercises.ordering: Order by customer_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use an aggregate subquery as the IN result set.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, warehouse_code, product_id, sku, and quantity_on_hand for inventory rows whose product_id appears in at least one return with status requested or approved.
exercises.ordering: Order by warehouse_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine IN, joins, grouping, and HAVING to filter aggregated results.
exercises.statement: Return category_id, category_name, and active_product_count for categories that contain at least one active product reviewed by a customer from India. Include only categories with at least two active products.
exercises.ordering: Order by active_product_count descending, then category_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a nested subquery to filter a multi-table transaction result.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, ordered_at, customer_id, customer_first_name, customer_last_name, and order_item_count for orders placed by customers who have also submitted at least one review with a rating of 5. Include only orders containing at least two line items.
exercises.ordering: Order by ordered_at descending, then order_id descending.

modules.title: ANY
modules.description: Compare a value against one or more values returned by a subquery.

exercises.learning_objective: Compare a value to any row returned by a subquery.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, sku, name, and unit_price for active products priced higher than at least one active product supplied by supplier_id 1.
exercises.ordering: Order by unit_price ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use = ANY as an alternative membership comparison.
exercises.statement: Return payment_id, order_id, payment_method, amount, and status for payments whose payment_method is used by at least one failed payment.
exercises.ordering: Order by payment_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a correlated ANY subquery to compare values within a group.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, product_name, category_id, and unit_price for active products that cost more than at least one other active product in the same category.
exercises.ordering: Order by category_id ascending, unit_price descending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine ANY with joins and correlated aggregate logic.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, warehouse_code, product_id, sku, and quantity_on_hand for inventory rows whose quantity_on_hand is greater than the reorder_point of at least one other inventory row in the same warehouse.
exercises.ordering: Order by warehouse_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use ANY in a grouped query with a related-table filter.
exercises.statement: Return supplier_id, supplier_name, and active_product_count for suppliers with at least two active products, where at least one active product costs more than any active product in category_id 1.
exercises.ordering: Order by active_product_count descending, then supplier_id ascending.

modules.title: ALL
modules.description: Compare a value against every value returned by a subquery.

exercises.learning_objective: Compare a value to every row returned by a subquery.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, sku, name, and unit_price for active products priced higher than every active product supplied by supplier_id 1.
exercises.ordering: Order by unit_price ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use = ALL to select rows whose related values all meet a condition.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, ordered_at, and status for orders whose every order item has a quantity of at least 2. Exclude orders with no order items.
exercises.ordering: Order by ordered_at descending, then order_id descending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a correlated ALL subquery to find a group maximum.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, product_name, category_id, and unit_price for active products whose unit_price is at least as high as every other active product in the same category. Include ties.
exercises.ordering: Order by category_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine ALL with joins and a correlated comparison across related rows.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, warehouse_code, product_id, sku, quantity_on_hand, and reorder_point for inventory rows whose quantity_on_hand is at least as high as every inventory quantity for the same product in other warehouses. Include ties.
exercises.ordering: Order by product_id ascending, then warehouse_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use ALL with grouping and a correlated aggregate subquery.
exercises.statement: Return supplier_id, supplier_name, product_id, product_name, and unit_price for active products whose unit_price is at least as high as every other active product from the same supplier. Include only suppliers with at least two active products, and include ties.
exercises.ordering: Order by supplier_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

modules.title: CTE
modules.description: Structure complex queries with named intermediate result sets.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a CTE to name an aggregate value for a comparison.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, sku, name, and unit_price for active products priced above the average unit_price of all active products.
exercises.ordering: Order by unit_price descending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a CTE to calculate reusable line-item totals.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, ordered_at, status, and item_total for orders with an item_total of at least 1,000.00. item_total is the sum of quantity multiplied by unit_price across each order's line items.
exercises.ordering: Order by item_total descending, then order_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a CTE to isolate a filtered data set before joining it to reference tables.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, warehouse_code, product_id, sku, quantity_on_hand, and reorder_point for inventory rows at or below their reorder point, but only for active products.
exercises.ordering: Order by warehouse_id ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine multiple CTEs with joins and aggregates.
exercises.statement: Return category_id, category_name, active_product_count, and average_rating for categories with at least two active products and at least one review. average_rating is the average rating across reviews of active products in the category.
exercises.ordering: Order by average_rating descending, then category_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use multiple CTEs to compare grouped business metrics to an overall benchmark.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, email, first_name, last_name, delivered_order_count, and delivered_item_total for customers whose delivered_item_total is above the average delivered_item_total among customers with at least one delivered order. delivered_item_total is the sum of quantity multiplied by unit_price for delivered orders.
exercises.ordering: Order by delivered_item_total descending, then customer_id ascending.

modules.title: RECURSIVE CTE
modules.description: Traverse hierarchical data through recursive common table expressions.

exercises.learning_objective: Traverse a category hierarchy from each root to its descendants.
exercises.statement: Return category_id, category_name, parent_category_id, hierarchy_level, and category_path for every category. hierarchy_level starts at 0 for root categories. category_path contains category names from the root to the category, separated by ' > '.
exercises.ordering: Order by category_path ascending, then category_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use a recursive CTE to list all direct and indirect reports of one employee.
exercises.statement: Return employee_id, first_name, last_name, manager_id, and hierarchy_level for employee_id 1 and every employee who reports to them directly or indirectly. hierarchy_level is 0 for employee_id 1.
exercises.ordering: Order by hierarchy_level ascending, then employee_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Build an employee hierarchy path while traversing a self-referencing table.
exercises.statement: Return employee_id, first_name, last_name, manager_id, hierarchy_level, and reporting_path for every employee. reporting_path contains employee IDs from the hierarchy root to the employee, separated by ' > '.
exercises.ordering: Order by reporting_path ascending, then employee_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine recursive traversal with joins and filtering on related business data.
exercises.statement: Return employee_id, first_name, last_name, hierarchy_level, warehouse_code, and warehouse_name for every direct or indirect report of employee_id 1 who works in a warehouse located in India. Exclude employee_id 1.
exercises.ordering: Order by hierarchy_level ascending, then employee_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine recursive traversal, joins, and aggregation to summarize a hierarchy.
exercises.statement: Return root_category_id, root_category_name, category_id, category_name, hierarchy_level, and active_product_count for every category in each root category's hierarchy. active_product_count is the number of active products assigned directly to that category, including zero counts.
exercises.ordering: Order by root_category_id ascending, hierarchy_level ascending, then category_id ascending.

modules.title: WINDOW FUNCTIONS
modules.description: Calculate rankings, running totals, comparisons with nearby rows, and partition-level metrics without collapsing detail rows.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine grouped order totals with a partitioned running total window.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, order_id, ordered_at, item_total, and running_delivered_item_total for every delivered order. item_total is the sum of quantity multiplied by unit_price for the order's line items. running_delivered_item_total is the cumulative item_total for that customer through the current delivered order, ordered by ordered_at and then order_id.
exercises.ordering: Order by customer_id ascending, ordered_at ascending, then order_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Rank aggregated related-table metrics within a partition while retaining rows with zero activity.
exercises.statement: Return category_id, category_name, product_id, sku, delivered_quantity, and delivered_quantity_rank for every active product. delivered_quantity is the total quantity of that product in delivered orders, including 0 for products with no delivered sales. delivered_quantity_rank is the dense rank within the product's category, with the highest delivered_quantity ranked 1 and ties sharing a rank.
exercises.ordering: Order by category_id ascending, delivered_quantity_rank ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use LAG to compare each row with the preceding chronological row in its partition.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, order_id, ordered_at, previous_order_id, previous_ordered_at, and time_since_previous_order for every order except each customer's first order. previous_order_id and previous_ordered_at identify the immediately preceding order for the same customer, ordered by ordered_at and then order_id. time_since_previous_order is ordered_at minus previous_ordered_at.
exercises.ordering: Order by customer_id ascending, ordered_at ascending, then order_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Apply multiple window calculations to grouped sales metrics and safely derive a partition share.
exercises.statement: Return category_id, category_name, product_id, sku, delivered_revenue, category_delivered_revenue, revenue_share_percent, and revenue_rank for every active product. delivered_revenue is the sum of quantity multiplied by unit_price for delivered orders, including 0 for products with no delivered sales. category_delivered_revenue is the delivered_revenue total for the product's category. revenue_share_percent is 100 multiplied by delivered_revenue divided by category_delivered_revenue, or NULL when the category total is 0. revenue_rank is the dense rank within the category by delivered_revenue descending, with ties sharing a rank.
exercises.ordering: Order by category_id ascending, revenue_rank ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine multi-table aggregation with a country-level dense rank and a rank filter.
exercises.statement: Return country_code, country_name, customer_id, email, delivered_order_count, delivered_item_total, and country_spend_rank for customers whose delivered_item_total ranks among the top two values in their country. Include ties. delivered_order_count is the number of delivered orders, and delivered_item_total is the sum of quantity multiplied by unit_price across their delivered order items.
exercises.ordering: Order by country_code ascending, country_spend_rank ascending, then customer_id ascending.

modules.title: CASE
modules.description: Classify business data with conditional expressions while combining prior query techniques.

exercises.learning_objective: Use CASE with an aggregated CTE to assign each customer a value tier.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, email, delivered_order_count, delivered_item_total, and customer_value_tier for every customer. delivered_item_total is the sum of quantity multiplied by unit_price across delivered orders. customer_value_tier is no_delivered_orders when delivered_order_count is 0, high_value when delivered_item_total is at least 50,000.00, and standard_value otherwise.
exercises.ordering: Order by delivered_item_total descending, then customer_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Classify inventory health from multiple aggregate conditions.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, sku, warehouse_count, low_stock_warehouse_count, and inventory_health for every active product. warehouse_count is the number of warehouses that stock the product. low_stock_warehouse_count is the number of those rows where quantity_on_hand is at or below reorder_point. inventory_health is not_stocked when warehouse_count is 0, reorder_needed when low_stock_warehouse_count is positive, and adequately_stocked otherwise.
exercises.ordering: Order by inventory_health ascending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Derive a fulfillment state from related order, payment, and shipment lifecycle states.
exercises.statement: Return order_id, order_status, payment_status, shipment_status, and fulfillment_state for every order. fulfillment_state is closed_or_returned when the order is cancelled or the shipment is returned; payment_issue when the payment failed; complete when the order is delivered and the payment completed; and awaiting_fulfillment otherwise.
exercises.ordering: Order by order_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use conditional logic with independently aggregated related metrics.
exercises.statement: Return supplier_id, supplier_name, active_product_count, returned_active_product_count, and return_exposure for every supplier. returned_active_product_count is the number of distinct active products from that supplier that appear in returns. return_exposure is no_active_product_returns when the returned count is 0, broad_return_exposure when it is at least the active_product_count, and limited_return_exposure otherwise.
exercises.ordering: Order by return_exposure ascending, then supplier_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine conditional labels with aggregate and window-based category benchmarks.
exercises.statement: Return category_id, category_name, product_id, sku, delivered_revenue, category_average_delivered_revenue, and revenue_performance for every active product. delivered_revenue is the sum of quantity multiplied by unit_price in delivered orders, including 0 for products with no delivered sales. category_average_delivered_revenue is the average delivered_revenue among active products in that category. revenue_performance is no_delivered_revenue when delivered_revenue is 0, above_category_average when it is above the category average, and at_or_below_category_average otherwise.
exercises.ordering: Order by category_id ascending, delivered_revenue descending, then product_id ascending.

modules.title: STRING FUNCTIONS
modules.description: Format, extract, concatenate, and aggregate text values in analytical queries.

exercises.learning_objective: Build hierarchical display text while traversing a recursive structure.
exercises.statement: Return category_id, category_name, parent_category_id, hierarchy_level, and category_path for every category. hierarchy_level is 0 for root categories. category_path contains names from the root to the category separated by ' > '. Use string construction as part of the recursive traversal.
exercises.ordering: Order by category_path ascending, then category_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Extract and format customer text attributes after filtering by a derived business metric.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, customer_name, email_username, email_domain, and delivered_item_total for customers whose delivered_item_total is above the average among customers with at least one delivered order. customer_name joins the first and last name with one space and uses title case. email_username and email_domain are the portions of email before and after '@'. delivered_item_total is the sum of quantity multiplied by unit_price across delivered orders.
exercises.ordering: Order by delivered_item_total descending, then customer_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use ordered string aggregation after multi-table grouping and filtering.
exercises.statement: Return supplier_id, supplier_name, category_id, category_name, active_product_count, and active_sku_list for each supplier-and-category combination with at least two active products. active_sku_list contains that combination's active SKUs separated by ', ' and sorted by SKU ascending.
exercises.ordering: Order by supplier_id ascending, then category_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Produce deterministic ordered text summaries from related review records.
exercises.statement: Return product_id, sku, review_count, reviewer_email_list, and review_text_summary for products with at least two reviews. reviewer_email_list contains reviewer emails separated by ', ' and sorted by email ascending. review_text_summary contains each review body, using '(no text)' for NULL bodies, separated by ' | ' and sorted by created_at ascending and then review_id ascending.
exercises.ordering: Order by review_count descending, then product_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Combine text formatting, window ranking, and string aggregation to summarize category leaders.
exercises.statement: Return category_id, category_name, category_code, top_delivered_revenue, and top_sku_list for every category with active products. category_code is the uppercase first three characters of category_name, followed by '-', followed by category_id padded to two digits. top_delivered_revenue is the highest delivered revenue among active products in the category, where delivered revenue is the sum of quantity multiplied by unit_price in delivered orders and includes 0 for products with no delivered sales. top_sku_list contains every active SKU tied for that revenue, separated by ' | ' and sorted by SKU ascending.
exercises.ordering: Order by category_id ascending.

modules.title: DATE FUNCTIONS
modules.description: Group, compare, and generate calendar-based metrics from timestamp data.

exercises.learning_objective: Group timestamped sales into calendar months and compare the grouped values to an overall benchmark.
exercises.statement: Return delivery_month, delivered_order_count, delivered_item_total, and average_monthly_delivered_item_total for every month containing delivered orders. delivery_month is the first calendar date of the month. delivered_item_total is the sum of quantity multiplied by unit_price across delivered orders in that month. average_monthly_delivered_item_total is the average delivered_item_total across the returned months.
exercises.ordering: Order by delivery_month ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use date extraction and aggregate boundaries to characterize a customer's delivery history.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, email, first_delivered_date, last_delivered_date, days_between_first_and_last_delivery, and delivered_order_count for customers with at least two delivered orders. first_delivered_date and last_delivered_date are calendar dates. days_between_first_and_last_delivery is the number of days from the first delivered order to the last.
exercises.ordering: Order by days_between_first_and_last_delivery descending, then customer_id ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Compare related timestamps and aggregate shipment timing by location and calendar day.
exercises.statement: Return warehouse_id, warehouse_code, delivery_date, delivered_shipment_count, and average_delivery_days for every warehouse-and-date combination with delivered shipments. delivery_date is the calendar date of delivered_at. average_delivery_days is the average number of days from shipped_at to delivered_at for that group.
exercises.ordering: Order by warehouse_id ascending, then delivery_date ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Generate a complete monthly calendar, fill missing sales periods, and compare each period to its predecessor.
exercises.statement: Return delivery_month, delivered_item_total, previous_month_delivered_item_total, and month_over_month_change for every calendar month from the earliest to the latest delivered order, including months with no delivered orders. delivery_month is the first calendar date of each month. delivered_item_total is 0 for a month with no delivered orders. previous_month_delivered_item_total is NULL for the first returned month, and month_over_month_change is delivered_item_total minus the previous month's total.
exercises.ordering: Order by delivery_month ascending.

exercises.learning_objective: Use LAG and date arithmetic to find closely spaced customer orders.
exercises.statement: Return customer_id, order_id, ordered_date, previous_order_id, previous_ordered_date, and days_since_previous_order for orders placed no more than 120 days after the previous order by the same customer. Determine the previous order chronologically by ordered_at and then order_id. ordered_date and previous_ordered_date are calendar dates.
exercises.ordering: Order by customer_id ascending, ordered_date ascending, then order_id ascending.
